skill.name: creating-skills
skill.description: Guides authors through creating Agent Skills with clear triggers, lean instructions, bundled resources, validation, and packaging steps.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and package Agent Skills with structured guidance and local helper scripts.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but review found the weak-crypto and Ruby backtick alerts are false positives from Markdown backticks, file extensions, and instructional text. The real risks are moderate: local scripts create and package files, and one reference promotes forceful prompt-control language for skill activation. No evidence found of malware, credential exfiltration, network execution, or commands built from untrusted input.

medium_findings.title: Prompt-Control Activation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The reference recommends forceful model-control language such as mandatory skill evaluation and critical activation instructions. This appears to be activation research rather than an attempt to override this audit, but marketplace users should treat it as prompt-influence content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly recommends words like MANDATORY and CRITICAL to alter model behavior. Confidence is high that this is prompt-control guidance, but intent appears instructional rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: The packaging script recursively adds every file under the selected skill folder to the generated archive. This is normal packaging behavior, but it can include secrets or unrelated files if the source folder is not clean.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The zip writer walks all files and writes them to the archive without an exclusion list. The risk depends on user folder contents, so this is a moderate operational concern.

low_findings.title: Local Scaffold Writes User-Specified Files
low_findings.description: The initializer creates a skill directory and writes template files under a user-provided output path. This is expected functionality and uses pathlib rather than shell commands, but users should choose the destination carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly creates directories and writes files at a path supplied on the command line. No command execution or network transfer is present, so the residual risk is low.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external-command and weak-crypto findings came from Markdown code fences, references to SKILL.md files, and instructional examples. No Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptographic use, or dynamic eval call was found in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences or prose examples, not executable Ruby, crypto, or eval code. Executable scripts use Python file and regex operations without dynamic code evaluation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt-Like Imperatives in Reference Material
dangerous_patterns.description: Reference content includes imperative phrases intended to make a model activate skills. This pattern can influence agent behavior if loaded into context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The intent to influence model activation is stated directly in the reference. It does not instruct the model to bypass safety or exfiltrate data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unfiltered Archive Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager writes all files from the skill directory into the archive. A clean source folder is required to avoid bundling unwanted local files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is clear and deterministic. The severity is limited because output is local and no upload behavior exists.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need structure, validation, and packaging guidance. This skill provides templates, review patterns, and local scripts for creating Agent Skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md frontmatter and body structure.
Guides authors through planning scripts, references, and assets.
Provides a local initializer for creating a starter skill directory.
Provides a local verifier for checking name, description, and file requirements.
Provides a local packager for creating a .skill archive after validation.
Includes reference material for progressive disclosure and review patterns.
limitations: It does not generate production-ready domain logic for a new skill.
It does not publish skills to a marketplace or remote registry.
The verifier checks basic structure, not full semantic quality.
The packager includes all files under the selected skill folder.

use_cases.title: Create a new reusable skill
use_cases.description: Use the scaffold and guidance to start a skill with required metadata and resource folders.

use_cases.title: Validate skill structure before sharing
use_cases.description: Run the verifier and adjust frontmatter, triggers, and required files before packaging.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing skill
use_cases.description: Use the design references to move details into resources and keep the main instructions focused.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a first skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new Agent Skill for [task]. Ask for the missing details, then scaffold the skill files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Author deciding what belongs in scripts, references, and assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill idea and recommend which instructions, scripts, references, and assets it should include.

prompt_templates.title: Review trigger quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md description for clear activation triggers, exclusions, and unnecessary workflow detail.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced author preparing a skill for distribution.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill folder, run the verifier, identify packaging risks, and prepare it for a .skill archive.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for rotating and merging PDF files.
output: A proposed skill name and trigger-focused description.
A folder plan with scripts for deterministic PDF operations.
Validation steps to run before packaging.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill description is too broad.
output: A concise diagnosis of trigger problems.
A revised description with clear use and non-use cases.
Notes about content that belongs in the body instead of metadata.

output_examples.input: Prepare this completed skill for sharing.
output: A list of files that will be included in the package.
Warnings about removing secrets or unrelated local files first.

best_practices: Keep the description focused on when the skill should activate.
Move long or specialized material into references loaded only when needed.
Inspect the skill folder before packaging to remove secrets and unrelated files.
anti_patterns: Do not put full workflow instructions into the frontmatter description.
Do not bundle large reference files unless the skill clearly needs them.
Do not package a working directory that contains private or temporary files.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Agent Skills with clear triggers, focused instructions, bundled resources, validation, and packaging.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it write files on my system?
faq.answer: Yes. The initializer creates folders and template files at the path you provide.

faq.question: Does it call external network services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network calls. The only URL found is a source reference in documentation.

faq.question: What should I check before packaging?
faq.answer: Check that the folder contains only intended skill files and no secrets, private notes, or temporary output.

faq.question: Can it judge the full quality of my skill?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and basic validation, but human review is still needed for usefulness and safety.
